name: Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)
name_original: Ruta de los Caleros – circular desde Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)
region_intro: Hiking around Montesclaros offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by rock formations, waterfalls, and extensive parklands. The region features varied terrain, from moderately challenging ascents to more straightforward paths through dense vegetation. Hikers can explore areas with significant elevation changes and panoramic viewpoints. The natural environment provides a range of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Montesclaros The most popular hiking route is Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26), an easy 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This circular path offers accessible terrain for a pleasant walk. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Portiña Reservoir and Segurilla Watchtower Trail, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This route features varied terrain and views of the reservoir and watchtower. Local hikers also love the Cruz de lo Llano – Riscal de Segurilla Mill loop from Segurilla, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Montesclaros is defined by iconic rock formations, numerous waterfalls, and diverse parklands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Montesclaros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Montesclaros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Montes Claros?
A: Montes Claros offers over a dozen hiking routes, catering to various preferences. You'll find a good mix of easy and moderate trails, with no difficult routes listed, making it accessible for many hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Montes Claros?
A: The region features diverse terrain, from moderately challenging ascents to more straightforward paths. You can expect to encounter iconic rock formations, dense vegetation, and areas with significant elevation changes, providing panoramic viewpoints. The natural environment includes extensive parklands and numerous waterfalls.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Montes Claros?
A: Yes, Montes Claros has several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018792785/route-of-the-caleros-circular-from-montesclaros-pr-to-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.2 km circular path. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de lo Llano loop from Segurilla&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.1 km trail.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Montes Claros?
A: Yes, Montes Claros offers several circular hiking routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018792785/route-of-the-caleros-circular-from-montesclaros-pr-to-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 7.2 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanzahíta – Lanzahíta loop from Lanzahíta&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.5 km long and also considered easy.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Montes Claros?
A: Montes Claros is rich in natural beauty. You can explore iconic rock formations like Pedra Santa and Morro da Boa Vista, which offer panoramic views. The region also boasts stunning waterfalls such as Cachoeira do Ipojuca. Additionally, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6116045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6117711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segurilla Watchtower&lt;/a&gt; on some trails.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible on hiking trails in Montes Claros?
A: Yes, the Montes Claros region is known for its waterfalls. Cachoeira do Ipojuca is a notable example, featuring several waterfalls along a trail through dense vegetation. While the first waterfall is easily accessible, subsequent falls offer more challenging treks and refreshing dips. Other waterfalls like Cachoeira do Sussuapara and Cachoeira da Vila Velha also provide rewarding experiences for those seeking natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Montes Claros?
A: The hiking trails in Montes Claros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the rewarding views from ascents, and the opportunity to immerse themselves in lush natural environments.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Hikes to iconic rock formations like Pedra Santa and Morro da Boa Vista culminate in breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding landscape and the city below. The Serra do Rola-Moça mountain range also provides expansive vistas from its peaks.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Montes Claros?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018792785/route-of-the-caleros-circular-from-montesclaros-pr-to-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31462854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de lo Llano – Riscal de Segurilla Mill loop from Segurilla&lt;/a&gt;, can take about 3 hours.

Q: Are there any parks suitable for hiking in Montes Claros?
A: Yes, Montes Claros features several parks ideal for hiking. The Parque Natural Metropolitano offers accessible paths through diverse ecosystems, perfect for families. Parque Municipal Milton Prates provides serene walking trails, while nearby Parque Nacional Cavernas do Peruacu and Parque Estadual da Lapa Grande are excellent for those who enjoy national parks and their natural attractions.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: While the region is primarily known for its natural landscapes, you can find some historical points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6117711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segurilla Watchtower&lt;/a&gt; is a man-made monument that can be seen on some trails, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Franciscan Convent of Velada&lt;/a&gt; is another nearby attraction worth noting.
tips: Very close to the center of the town of Montesclaros is the hermitage of San Sebastián with an interesting history and which, in turn, is surrounded by a very green and picturesque landscape.

The area surrounding the town of Montesclaros is known for its marble deposits that have been exploited for its extraction. Today, many of them are no longer in use and can be visited by following the circular marked itinerary that crosses a beautiful landscape with lots of greenery and a very picturesque setting.
